Here's what each one does and how I actually use it. J-147 J-147 is the newest of the three. Most people in the research world have heard of dihexa and noopept, but J-147 is still pretty novel. It's a synthetic derivative of curcumin, developed at the Salk Institute as a candidate Alzheimer's drug. The big advantage over regular curcumin is bioavailability. reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Hunter Williams (2026-03-29; the-best-nootropic-you-dont-know-about-j-147-dihexa-and-noopept-explained--dded4390 13:54; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/the-best-nootropic-you-dont-know-about-j-147-dihexa-and-noopept-explained--dded4390/): "Many people like it around 10 to 30 milligrams per day." Hunter Williams (2026-03-29; the-best-nootropic-you-dont-know-about-j-147-dihexa-and-noopept-explained--dded4390 33:45; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/the-best-nootropic-you-dont-know-about-j-147-dihexa-and-noopept-explained--dded4390/): "If you use two, it'd be 10mg. first thing in the morning, two to three days per week, and then on the non-U stage, use 600 milligrams of Alpha GPC."
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- Hunter Williams (2026-03-29; the-best-nootropic-you-dont-know-about-j-147-dihexa-and-noopept-explained--dded4390 26:17; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/the-best-nootropic-you-dont-know-about-j-147-dihexa-and-noopept-explained--dded4390/): "Now, potential side effects, we could have slight anxiety or restlessness occur in very sensitive individuals, where others find it actually reduces anxiety. Again, this is where neutropics, two people could take it and have completely opposite effects."
- Hunter Williams (2026-03-29; the-best-nootropic-you-dont-know-about-j-147-dihexa-and-noopept-explained--dded4390 26:51; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/the-best-nootropic-you-dont-know-about-j-147-dihexa-and-noopept-explained--dded4390/): "There are also no known dangerous interactions, however, because NUPEP has mild MAO inhibiting effects in the cortex from some studies, one should be cautious combining it with other MAOs inhibitors or stimulants to avoid blood pressure spikes or agitation."
- Hunter Williams (2026-03-29; the-best-nootropic-you-dont-know-about-j-147-dihexa-and-noopept-explained--dded4390 31:03; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/the-best-nootropic-you-dont-know-about-j-147-dihexa-and-noopept-explained--dded4390/): "If we were to use this every single day, eventually what could happen is we could desensitize receptors. We could drive too much synaptic growth. we can raise neurotrophin levels beyond the optimal. BNF is a Goldilocks molecule. Too little is bad, but too may disrupt emotional stability and cause excitability or anxiety."

chronic.dose: Hunter Williams (2026-03-29; the-best-nootropic-you-dont-know-about-j-147-dihexa-and-noopept-explained--dded4390 13:22; https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/the-best-nootropic-you-dont-know-about-j-147-dihexa-and-noopept-explained--dded4390/): "A small open-label clinical study in Russia followed 56 days of NUPEP, where they took 10 milligrams twice daily in patients with mild cognitive impairment."
